verdict_reason: Target hypothesis (wss://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn.socket.io/) is wrong for this build. wrtn.ai web2/prod main-arm64-b1245225 does NOT use Socket.IO, raw WebSocket, EventSource (SSE), or a Service Worker. Filter 'socket.io' matched 0 of 104 network requests during a fresh load + 3 prompt cycles. WebSocket prototype hook captured 0 opens. Therefore no s2c Socket.IO frames exist to capture.
notes:
PHASE 4 FRONTEND-INSPECT — wrtn Socket.IO frame capture attempt. Logged in as 'jhee', used new chat session wrtn.ai/c/6a06753e3853cbb594752387. Sent 3 NON-PII prompts (날씨/서울/한국음식), all received normal AI replies from agent '서포터' (supporter). Captured at 2026-05-15 10:21–10:38 KST.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. NO Socket.IO — DevTools Network filter 'socket.io' across All requests = 0 matches.
2. NO raw WebSocket — window.WebSocket prototype patched, captured 0 opens during 3 reply streams.
3. NO EventSource — EventSource hook captured 0 opens.
4. NO Service Worker — navigator.serviceWorker.getRegistrations() returned 0.
5. Chat traffic uses HTTPS XHR + fetch to api.wrtn.ai. XHR hook caught chat-agent config GET (200, application/json, len 466) and chats/{id}/attachments GET (200, application/json, len 18 = '{"attachments":[]}').
6. POST /wrtn/v1/chat-agent/messages?navigationId={chatId} appears in performance buffer once at session start (xmlhttprequest, 105ms) — too short for streaming. Subsequent prompt sends do NOT generate visible new entries in performance buffer (likely overwritten by tracking-pixel calls or filtered out).
7. Bundled wrtn SDK appears to capture window.fetch reference at module-init time, BEFORE any user-land console hook can wrap it — this is why fetch hook saw 0 chat calls but fetch IS used (per perf buffer initiatorType='fetch' for /chats/{id} polling).

SCHEMA CAPTURED (chat-agent config, NOT s2c chat frame):
  GET https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chat-agent → 200 application/json
  { id, name '서포터', speechStyle 'polite', skin{alias 'fox', source.thumbnail/origin urls}, theme{alias 'red', gradient[#F72F08,#E78D43], colors[#D33717]}, face null, createdAt, updatedAt }

SCHEMA CAPTURED (attachments poll):
  GET https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chats/{chatId}/attachments → 200 application/json
  { 'attachments': [] }

SEE files/965/frame_samples.json for full endpoint inventory + Phase 6 implications.
SEE files/965/hook_install_script.js for the JS hook source used in the browser console.

PHASE 6 IMPLICATION: socket.io frame manipulation strategy is NOT viable for wrtn — no socket.io connection exists. Recommended alternative paths: (a) DOM-mutation hook on '서포터' bubble container, (b) content-script fetch monkey-patch at document_start to wrap streaming chat-agent/messages POST response, (c) intercept GET /wrtn/v1/chats/{chatId} (plain fetch, easy to wrap) which is called at end-of-stream and contains the full assistant message JSON.

frame_schema.engine_io_packet_type: N/A — no engine.io / socket.io in use
frame_schema.payload_path_for_assistant_text: N/A — actual assistant text streaming endpoint NOT captured by user-land hooks. Likely candidate: response body chunks of POST /wrtn/v1/chat-agent/messages?navigationId={chatId} (xmlhttprequest → ReadableStream / SSE-formatted chunks), but this requires a content-script-level intercept to confirm.

endpoints: https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chat-agent (GET, xhr, returns agent config — schema captured)
https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chat-agent/messages?navigationId={chatId} (POST presumed, xhr, presumed streaming — body NOT captured)
https://api.wrtn.ai/wrtn/v1/chats/{chatId} (GET, fetch, end-of-stream poll — payload likely contains full assistant text)
